instruction:
Randomly selected $130$ student cars have ages with a mean of $7.6$ years and a standard deviation of $3.6$ years, while randomly selected $95$ faculty cars have ages with a mean of $5.3$ years and a standard deviation of $3.3$ years. 1. $\ $ Use a $0.03$ significance level to test the claim that student cars are older than faculty cars. The test statistic is [ANS]
The critical value is [ANS]
Is there sufficient evidence to support the claim that student cars are older than faculty cars? [ANS] A. Yes  B. No
2. $\ $ Construct a $97$ \% confidence interval estimate of the difference $\mu_1-\mu_2$, where $\mu_1$ is the mean age of student cars and $\mu_2$ is the mean age of faculty cars. [ANS] $< (\mu_1-\mu_2) <$ [ANS]
